title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

seo.imageAlt: Perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak basted with garlic butter, served with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in a tender, flavorful New York Strip Steak, expertly cooked and infused with rich garlic butter.

description: Indulge in a tender, flavorful New York Strip Steak, expertly cooked and infused with rich garlic butter. This classic American dish delivers a restaurant-quality experience right in your home, perfect for a special dinner or an elevated weeknight meal. Enjoy the perfect sear, juicy interior, and aromatic garlic butter basting.

ingredients.notes: about 1.5 inches thick, approximately 1.5 lbs total
or other high smoke point oil like grapeseed or avocado

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the New York Strip Steaks from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes before cooking. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Allowing the steaks to come closer to room temperature promotes more even cooking.

instructions.title: Heat the Skillet
instructions.text: Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Add the olive oil and heat until it begins to shimmer and lightly smoke. This indicates the pan is hot enough for a good sear.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep, golden-brown crust forms. Use tongs to sear the edges as well, about 1 minute per edge.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter and Aromatics
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the unsalted butter, smashed garlic cloves, and fresh rosemary sprigs to the skillet. As the butter melts, tilt the pan slightly and use a large spoon to baste the melting garlic butter over the steaks continuously for 3-5 minutes, ensuring all surfaces are coated.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking to Desired Doneness
instructions.text: Continue basting until the steaks reach your desired internal temperature. For medium-rare, aim for 125-130F; for medium, 130-135F. For thicker steaks, you may transfer the skillet to a preheated 400F oven for 5-10 minutes after basting, if needed, to reach the desired doneness.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked steaks to a cutting board and let them rest for at least 10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a more tender and juicy steak. Slice against the grain and spoon any remaining pan sauce over the top before serving.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: For an authentic American steakhouse sear, ensure your cast iron skillet is smoking hot before adding the steak. This creates that desirable, flavorful crust.
While rosemary is a classic choice, fresh thyme or even a bay leaf can be substituted for a slightly different aromatic profile in your garlic butter basting.
Always let your steak rest for at least 10 minutes after cooking. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a more tender and flavorful bite.
article:
Savoring a perfectly cooked steak is one of life's simple, yet profound, pleasures. And when that steak is a New York Strip, infused with the rich, aromatic embrace of garlic butter, you've got a dish that transcends mere sustenance to become a memorable culinary experience. The New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a quintessential American dinner recipe, beloved for its robust flavor, satisfying texture, and the sheer elegance it brings to any table. Whether you're a seasoned home chef or looking to impress with a special meal, mastering this classic is a rewarding endeavor.

## What is New York Strip Steak?

The New York Strip, also known as Kansas City Strip, Ambassador Steak, or simply Strip Steak, is a premium cut of beef taken from the short loin of the cow. It's boneless, though bone-in versions (often called Kansas City Strip) exist and offer even more flavor. This cut is characterized by its fine grain, a good balance of tenderness and chewiness, and a distinctive band of fat along one side (the 'fat cap') which renders down during cooking, adding incredible flavor and moisture.

Unlike the super-tender filet mignon or the heavily marbled ribeye, the New York Strip offers a superb middle ground. It boasts a strong beefy flavor without being overly fatty, making it a favorite among steak connoisseurs. Its consistent shape and thickness also make it ideal for pan-searing, grilling, or broiling, allowing for an even cook and a beautiful crust.

## Ingredients for Garlic Butter Strip Steak

Crafting the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter requires quality ingredients, but the list is surprisingly short and simple. Focus on fresh, high-quality components for the best results.

For the Steak:
*   2 (10-12 oz each) New York Strip Steaks, 1.5 inches thick
*   1-2 tablespoons high smoke point oil (like grapeseed, canola, or avocado oil)
*   Coarse sea salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

For the Garlic Butter:
*   4 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   4-6 cloves garlic, minced
*   2 sprigs fresh rosemary or thyme (or a mix), optional but highly recommended
*   Pinch of red pepper flakes, optional (for a hint of heat)

## How to Cook New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

Mastering the art of cooking New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is simpler than you might think, especially when pan-searing. The key is high heat, proper seasoning, and a luscious garlic butter finish.

1.  **Prepare the Steaks:** Remove steaks from the refrigerator at least 30-60 minutes before cooking to allow them to come closer to room temperature. This promotes more even cooking. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels; moisture prevents a good sear. Season generously on all sides with coarse sea salt and freshly ground black pepper. Don't be shy with the salt; it's crucial for flavor and crust formation.
2.  **Heat the Pan:** Place a heavy-bottomed skillet (cast iron is ideal) over high heat until it's smoking slightly. Add the high smoke point oil to the pan. The oil should shimmer, indicating it’s hot enough.
3.  **Sear the Steaks:** Carefully place the steaks in the hot pan. You should hear a vigorous sizzle. Do not move them for 2-4 minutes, allowing a deep golden-brown crust to form. Flip the steaks and sear the other side for another 2-4 minutes.
4.  **Add the Garlic Butter:** Reduce the heat to medium. Add the unsalted butter, minced garlic, and fresh herbs (if using) to the pan. As the butter melts, it will sizzle and become fragrant.
5.  **Baste:** Tilt the pan slightly and use a spoon to continuously baste the melting garlic butter over the steaks for 1-2 minutes. This infuses the steak with incredible flavor and helps finish the cooking.
6.  **Check Doneness:** Use an instant-read thermometer to check the internal temperature.
    *   Rare: 125-130°F (52-54°C)
    *   Medium-Rare: 130-135°F (54-57°C)
    *   Medium: 135-140°F (57-60°C)
    *   Medium-Well: 140-145°F (60-63°C)
    *   Well-Done: 150°F+ (65°C+)
    Remember, the temperature will rise a few degrees while resting.
7.  **Rest the Steaks:** Transfer the cooked steaks to a cutting board or plate. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a tender, juicy result.

## Tips for Perfect Pan-Seared Steak

Achieving that restaurant-quality sear and juicy interior for your New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter isn't difficult with a few key techniques:

*   **Room Temperature is Key:** Cold steaks thrown into a hot pan cook unevenly. The outside burns before the inside warms up.
*   **Pat Dry, Always:** Moisture is the enemy of a good crust. Ensure your steaks are as dry as possible to achieve that coveted Maillard reaction (browning).
*   **High Heat:** Start with a very hot pan. This creates the initial sear and crust. Don't be afraid of a little smoke!
*   **Don't Overcrowd:** Cook one or two steaks at a time, depending on your pan size. Overcrowding lowers the pan temperature and steams the meat instead of searing it.
*   **Use a Thermometer:** Visual cues are helpful, but an instant-read thermometer is your best friend for precise doneness.
*   **Resting is Non-Negotiable:** This is arguably the most important step for a juicy steak. Skipping it means all those delicious juices will run out onto your plate.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

The rich flavor of New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter pairs beautifully with a variety of sides, creating a well-rounded and satisfying meal.

*   **Classic Comfort:** Creamy mashed potatoes or crispy roasted potatoes are always a winner. A side of asparagus, green beans, or broccoli, simply steamed or roasted, provides a fresh contrast.
*   **Heartier Sides:** Consider a rich mushroom risotto, a hearty baked potato with all the fixings, or a vibrant corn on the cob.
*   **Salads:** A light, crisp green salad with a vinaigrette dressing can cut through the richness of the steak.
*   **Wine Pairings:** For red wine lovers, a bold Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, or Zinfandel would complement the robust flavor of the steak. If you prefer white, a full-bodied Chardonnay could also work.
*   **Beer Pairings:** A rich porter, a malty brown ale, or even a crisp lager can be excellent companions.

## Variations and Substitutions

While this recipe focuses on the classic New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, there's always room for personalization.

*   **Herb Power:** Experiment with different fresh herbs in your garlic butter. Sage, oregano, or even a touch of fresh marjoram can add unique aromatic notes.
*   **Spice It Up:** For those who like a kick, increase the red pepper flakes or add a pinch of cayenne pepper to the garlic butter.
*   **Compound Butter:** Instead of basting, you can prepare a compound butter ahead of time. Mix softened butter with minced garlic, herbs, salt, and pepper, then roll it into a log and chill. Slice off a medallion and place it on the hot steak as it rests, allowing it to melt and seep into the meat.
*   **Different Fats:** While butter is king for flavor, you can start your sear with beef tallow or clarified butter for an even richer crust.
*   **Steak Cuts:** While the New York Strip is perfect here, this garlic butter method can also elevate other cuts like ribeye, sirloin, or even a thicker cut of flank steak (though cooking times will vary).

Creating a magnificent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a testament to simple ingredients handled with care. The deep, savory notes of the seared beef, the pungent warmth of the garlic, and the luxurious silkiness of the butter combine to form a dish that is both comforting and sophisticated. It’s an American dinner recipe that promises to be a showstopper, delivering an unforgettable dining experience every time. So next time you're craving a truly exceptional meal, reach for the ingredients to create your own unforgettable New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter.
